Decomposition Analysis Demo

Yu Chen, José Manuel Aburto

2026-03-12

Preamble

The aim of this exercise is to apply the linear integral model proposed by Horiuchi et al (2008) and stepwise replacement method proposed by Andreev et al. (2002). The example is applied to life expectancy for Venezuelan males.

The aim is to quantify the effect of age-specific mortality changes on life expectancy between 1996 and 2013, and quantify the contribution of cause of death (CoD).

The CoD object contains life table and cause of death data for the years 1996 and 2013. Names 1:10 are ten independent, cause-of-death groups.

You can choose any two years that you want to do the decomposition.

Installation

# TODO: install the packages if you haven't already
# install.packages("DemoDecomp")
# install.packages("ggpubr")
require(ggplot2)
require(tidyr)
require(DemoDecomp)

Load data and source functions

load('Decomp_Data_L4.RData')

We already have a function for life expectancy and for the decomp algorithm

#load some functions and some info for graphs
source('Functions_5.R')

Decomposition Analyses for life expectancy

Set up

Following the method, we require two vectors of rates: one for 2013 and one for 1996. You can choose any two years that you want to do the decomposition, but make sure to change the ‘ref.yr’ and ‘tar.yr’ variables in the code below.

# TODO: note to change
ref.yr <- 1996
tar.yr <- 2013

# we need age-cause specific mortality rates in each CoD
COD1 <- as.matrix(
  cause.age.specific.mx[cause.age.specific.mx$year==ref.yr,
                        1:10]
  )


COD2 <- as.matrix(
  cause.age.specific.mx[cause.age.specific.mx$year==tar.yr,
                        1:10]
  )

Question: How to set the function for the decomposition by CoD?

Recall what we learnt yesterday

e0.frommx
## function (nmx = mx, sex = 1, age = c(0, 1, seq(5, 85, 5)), nax = NULL) 
## {
##     n <- c(diff(age), 999)
##     if (is.null(nax)) {
##         nax <- 0.5 * n
##         if (n[2] == 4) {
##             if (sex == 1) {
##                 if (nmx[1] >= 0.107) {
##                   nax[1] <- 0.33
##                   nax[2] <- 1.352
##                 }
##                 else {
##                   nax[1] <- 0.045 + 2.684 * nmx[1]
##                   nax[2] <- 1.651 - 2.816 * nmx[1]
##                 }
##             }
##             if (sex == 2) {
##                 if (nmx[1] >= 0.107) {
##                   nax[1] <- 0.35
##                   nax[2] <- 1.361
##                 }
##                 else {
##                   nax[1] <- 0.053 + 2.8 * nmx[1]
##                   nax[2] <- 1.522 - 1.518 * nmx[1]
##                 }
##             }
##         }
##     }
##     nqx <- (n * nmx)/(1 + (n - nax) * nmx)
##     nqx <- c(nqx[-(length(nqx))], 1)
##     nqx[nqx > 1] <- 1
##     npx <- 1 - nqx
##     lx <- cumprod(c(1, npx))
##     ndx <- -diff(lx)
##     lxpn <- lx[-1]
##     nLxpn <- n * lxpn + ndx * nax
##     nLx <- c(nLxpn[-length(nLxpn)], lxpn[length(lxpn) - 1]/nmx[length(nmx)])
##     Tx <- rev(cumsum(rev(nLx)))
##     lx <- lx[1:length(age)]
##     ex <- Tx/lx
##     e0 <- ex[1]
##     return(e0)
## }

TRICK: What we need is then a function that gives a long vector with the same order as the input of the decomposition, which is a vector of age-cause specific mortality.

e0frommxc <- function(mxcvec,sex=1){
  dim(mxcvec) <- c(19,length(mxcvec)/19)
  mx          <- rowSums(mxcvec)
  e0.frommx(mx,sex)
}

QUESTION:

- Why do we need to set the function like this?

- What does the ‘dim’ function do in this case?

- What does the ‘rowSums’ function do in this case?

- What does the number 19 mean in this case?

Horiuchi decomposition

Now use the Horiuchi decomposition. First set your N input, which is the number of steps in the decomposition. The higher the N, the more accurate the decomposition, but it will take more time to run.

# TODO: note to change
N.input <- 1e3 # you can try 10, 100, 1e4...

# horiuchi decomposition
horiuchi.results.raw <- horiuchi(
  func = e0frommxc, 
  pars1 = c(COD1), 
  pars2 = c(COD2), 
  N = N.input)

dim(horiuchi.results.raw) <- dim(COD1)
horiuchi.results.raw
##                [,1]          [,2]          [,3]          [,4]          [,5]
##  [1,]  4.217918e-03  0.0008808139  7.420893e-04  0.0098113760  0.0019523740
##  [2,]  4.289051e-03  0.0076390278  5.336350e-04 -0.0008564005  0.0127357267
##  [3,]  4.003514e-03  0.0022921593 -1.112929e-04 -0.0039980231  0.0172048403
##  [4,]  2.960660e-03  0.0038052664  3.035201e-05  0.0002222279  0.0086199839
##  [5,]  4.590370e-03 -0.0001051925  1.216235e-03 -0.3015918245 -0.0398756721
##  [6,] -8.664124e-04  0.0016863483 -4.726468e-04 -0.4632924148 -0.0690744470
##  [7,]  2.747243e-03  0.0010329431 -2.025241e-03 -0.2829054398 -0.0317993034
##  [8,] -5.270914e-05 -0.0001320688 -8.731374e-03 -0.1592305823 -0.0071370873
##  [9,]  1.055335e-03  0.0099783420 -4.592429e-03 -0.0765550520  0.0003143796
## [10,]  3.817584e-02  0.0023606491 -5.594205e-03 -0.0398057874  0.0146184546
## [11,]  1.180051e-02  0.0064653730  4.069769e-03 -0.0225122460  0.0471929105
## [12,]  3.009605e-02 -0.0025946048 -1.446968e-02 -0.0287881318  0.0120136887
## [13,]  8.301949e-02 -0.0027903949 -2.391967e-02 -0.0067918616  0.0175178172
## [14,]  1.171585e-01  0.0286319294 -3.134130e-02 -0.0090071668  0.0187071827
## [15,]  2.181542e-01  0.0531835143 -2.665328e-02 -0.0006441078  0.0250790837
## [16,]  2.024715e-01  0.0485086148 -2.326337e-02 -0.0028976748  0.0137718486
## [17,]  1.880153e-01  0.0254266154 -3.386940e-02 -0.0008388516  0.0146838976
## [18,]  2.100875e-01  0.0419738175 -2.250179e-02  0.0009107818  0.0129310166
## [19,]  1.786467e-01  0.0084376208 -2.809708e-02 -0.0033823199  0.0101401524
##                [,6]          [,7]          [,8]          [,9]       [,10]
##  [1,]  0.1097174968  0.0706157053  0.2606945988  0.0074810648  0.08246479
##  [2,] -0.0002368611  0.0319294063  0.0835099146  0.0033815484  0.09552860
##  [3,]  0.0002341413  0.0014972917  0.0039268872  0.0010211005  0.02929992
##  [4,]  0.0000000000  0.0017560442  0.0014458803  0.0007823514  0.01666635
##  [5,]  0.0000000000  0.0031936666 -0.0040274592  0.0045486990  0.02365894
##  [6,]  0.0000000000 -0.0021415907 -0.0010702986 -0.0007376572  0.02436131
##  [7,]  0.0000000000 -0.0055070023  0.0079388735  0.0029499776  0.02754503
##  [8,]  0.0000000000 -0.0082727710 -0.0008382554 -0.0025211336  0.01352497
##  [9,]  0.0000000000 -0.0123286350 -0.0039552819  0.0077919956  0.01739271
## [10,]  0.0000000000 -0.0057654927 -0.0034088076  0.0053568525  0.01850573
## [11,]  0.0000000000 -0.0071013585  0.0257760374  0.0049976578 -0.02347659
## [12,]  0.0000000000 -0.0031151049 -0.0022749313  0.0008231922  0.01448278
## [13,]  0.0000000000  0.0009171578  0.0047918538 -0.0019511499  0.01970460
## [14,]  0.0000000000  0.0015026293  0.0233860931  0.0100151885  0.02209355
## [15,]  0.0000000000  0.0237949195  0.0290272127  0.0171852687  0.03759757
## [16,]  0.0000000000  0.0137393554  0.0310951094  0.0132366927  0.05497952
## [17,]  0.0000000000  0.0273950830  0.0349830213  0.0157237317  0.04509706
## [18,]  0.0000000000  0.0364675190  0.0460162749  0.0250803253  0.06553140
## [19,]  0.0000000000  0.0062578423  0.0364342858  0.0117926140  0.07581089

Check if our decomposed contributions sum up to the original difference…

original <- e0frommxc(COD2) - e0frommxc(COD1)
with.decomp <- sum(horiuchi.results.raw)
cat('\nThe difference between the sum of the decomposed contributions and the original difference is:', with.decomp - original, ' with the N used in the decomposition is', N.input)
## 
## The difference between the sum of the decomposed contributions and the original difference is: -2.389181e-08  with the N used in the decomposition is 1000

Restructure the data to have a nice dataset for graphing

horiuchi.results            <- data.frame(horiuchi.results.raw)
colnames(horiuchi.results)  <- cause_names
horiuchi.results$Age        <- age_names
horiuchi.results            <- gather(data = horiuchi.results,
                                      key = Cause,
                                      value = Contribution,
                                      -Age)

Figure: the results of the Horiuchi decomposition method

p.horiuchi <- 
  ggplot(data = horiuchi.results, 
         aes(x = factor(Age, 
                        levels = unique(horiuchi.results$Age)), 
             y = Contribution,
             fill = factor(Cause,
                           levels = unique(horiuchi.results$Cause)))) +
  ggtitle(bquote(~'Change in '~ e[0] ~'1996-2013, Horiuchi decomposition method' ))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(order = 1, ncol = 3)) +
  labs(x = "Age group", y = "Contribution", fill = "Cause") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust=1),
    axis.line        = element_blank(),
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 8),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )
p.horiuchi

Stepwise replacement decomposition

Now, we do the same but with the stepwise replacement method. This method is less accurate than the Horiuchi decomposition, but it is faster to run.

We first look at the function and there are three options to .

You can find the argument ‘direction’ in the ‘stepwise_replacement’ function. Now, choose the direction of decomposition you want to run. The default is ‘up’.

help(stepwise_replacement)
# TODO: note to change
dir.pick <- "both" # or "down", "up"

stepwise.results.raw <- stepwise_replacement(
  e0frommxc, 
  pars1 = c(COD1), 
  pars2 = c(COD2), 
  direction = dir.pick)

dim(stepwise.results.raw) <- dim(COD1)
stepwise.results.raw
##                [,1]          [,2]          [,3]          [,4]          [,5]
##  [1,]  4.222364e-03  0.0008805372  7.419635e-04  0.0098082417  0.0019518658
##  [2,]  4.293249e-03  0.0076359870  5.335063e-04 -0.0008560606  0.0127310344
##  [3,]  4.007739e-03  0.0022912010 -1.112652e-04 -0.0039963506  0.0171979868
##  [4,]  2.964041e-03  0.0038035393  3.034378e-05  0.0002221268  0.0086162282
##  [5,]  4.596090e-03 -0.0001051404  1.215875e-03 -0.3014338394 -0.0398569013
##  [6,] -8.676011e-04  0.0016854339 -4.724941e-04 -0.4630052302 -0.0690397898
##  [7,]  2.751407e-03  0.0010323193 -2.024508e-03 -0.2827117584 -0.0317818499
##  [8,] -5.279836e-05 -0.0001319792 -8.727790e-03 -0.1591125392 -0.0071326175
##  [9,]  1.057354e-03  0.0099706615 -4.590261e-03 -0.0764919172  0.0003141485
## [10,]  3.825897e-02  0.0023585557 -5.591121e-03 -0.0397679105  0.0146055294
## [11,]  1.183024e-02  0.0064586224  4.067110e-03 -0.0224867300  0.0471411620
## [12,]  3.018598e-02 -0.0025913279 -1.445809e-02 -0.0287480760  0.0119970720
## [13,]  8.331437e-02 -0.0027859707 -2.389480e-02 -0.0067798256  0.0174866079
## [14,]  1.176484e-01  0.0285730226 -3.129625e-02 -0.0089859030  0.0186626444
## [15,]  2.191547e-01  0.0530363914 -2.659661e-02 -0.0006419713  0.0249955130
## [16,]  2.033933e-01  0.0483164095 -2.318455e-02 -0.0028833060  0.0137037212
## [17,]  1.887090e-01  0.0252627440 -3.366336e-02 -0.0008320286  0.0145656353
## [18,]  2.097536e-01  0.0414954426 -2.223142e-02  0.0008973677  0.0127437646
## [19,]  1.731986e-01  0.0081736156 -2.705542e-02 -0.0032437250  0.0097348988
##                [,6]          [,7]          [,8]          [,9]       [,10]
##  [1,]  0.1096845276  0.0705890803  0.2606026509  0.0074804834  0.08247418
##  [2,] -0.0002367758  0.0319175273  0.0834822310  0.0033812372  0.09553497
##  [3,]  0.0002340503  0.0014966848  0.0039254642  0.0010209991  0.02930224
##  [4,]  0.0000000000  0.0017552729  0.0014453123  0.0007822672  0.01666780
##  [5,]  0.0000000000  0.0031921416 -0.0040257416  0.0045481751  0.02366124
##  [6,]  0.0000000000 -0.0021404700 -0.0010698007 -0.0007375665  0.02436405
##  [7,]  0.0000000000 -0.0055037619  0.0079347357  0.0029495591  0.02754847
##  [8,]  0.0000000000 -0.0082672115 -0.0008377596 -0.0025207147  0.01352688
##  [9,]  0.0000000000 -0.0123190401 -0.0039525923  0.0077904543  0.01739552
## [10,]  0.0000000000 -0.0057601979 -0.0034060942  0.0053555685  0.01850925
## [11,]  0.0000000000 -0.0070935066  0.0257515657  0.0049961871 -0.02348166
## [12,]  0.0000000000 -0.0031108596 -0.0022722990  0.0008228905  0.01448644
## [13,]  0.0000000000  0.0009155658  0.0047848711 -0.0019502243  0.01971131
## [14,]  0.0000000000  0.0014991763  0.0233418141  0.0100089608  0.02210470
## [15,]  0.0000000000  0.0237192235  0.0289535585  0.0171717099  0.03763104
## [16,]  0.0000000000  0.0136750343  0.0309831274  0.0132228961  0.05507938
## [17,]  0.0000000000  0.0271892973  0.0347898896  0.0157009230  0.04527553
## [18,]  0.0000000000  0.0359916493  0.0456009838  0.0250320318  0.06617918
## [19,]  0.0000000000  0.0060310912  0.0356082979  0.0117137896  0.07947590
with.decomp <- sum(stepwise.results.raw)
cat('\nThe difference between the sum of the stepwise replacement decomposed contributions and the original difference is:', with.decomp - original, ' with the direction used in the decomposition is', dir.pick)
## 
## The difference between the sum of the stepwise replacement decomposed contributions and the original difference is: 0  with the direction used in the decomposition is both

Rescrtructure the data to have a nice dataset for graphing

stepwise.results            <- data.frame(stepwise.results.raw)
colnames(stepwise.results)  <- cause_names
stepwise.results$Age        <- age_names
stepwise.results            <- gather(data = stepwise.results,
                                      key = Cause,
                                      value = Contribution,
                                      -Age)

Figure: the results of the Stepwise replacement decomposition

p.stepwise <- 
  ggplot(data = stepwise.results, 
         aes(x = factor(Age, 
                        levels = unique(stepwise.results$Age)), 
             y = Contribution, 
             fill = factor(Cause,
                           levels = unique(stepwise.results$Cause)))) +
  ggtitle(bquote(~'Change in '~ e[0] ~'1996-2013, Stepwise replacement method' ))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(order = 1, ncol = 3)) +
  labs(x = "Age group", y = "Contribution", fill = "Cause") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust=1),
    axis.line        = element_blank(),
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 8),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )
p.stepwise

Comparison of the two methods

p.both <-  ggpubr::ggarrange(p.horiuchi,
                             p.stepwise,
                             ncol = 1, 
                             labels = c('a', 'b'),
                             common.legend = TRUE,
                             legend = 'bottom',
                             align = 'hv',
                             widths = c(1,1),
                             heights = c(1,1))
p.both

Decomposition Analyses for lifespan variation

We can do the same for the standard deviation of the age at death distribution, which is a measure of lifespan variation. The function for the standard deviation of the age at death distribution is already defined in the Functions_5.R file. You can use the same code as before, but replace the function and the original difference with the standard deviation of the age at death distribution.

# change the previous function to sdfrommxc
horiuchi.results.sd <- horiuchi(
  func = sdfrommxc, 
  pars1 = c(COD1), 
  pars2 = c(COD2), 
  N = N.input)
dim(horiuchi.results.sd) <- dim(COD1)
horiuchi.results.sd
##                [,1]          [,2]          [,3]          [,4]          [,5]
##  [1,] -6.393545e-03 -1.335143e-03 -1.124863e-03 -1.487214e-02 -2.959420e-03
##  [2,] -6.233726e-03 -1.110260e-02 -7.755875e-04  1.244696e-03 -1.851016e-02
##  [3,] -5.316449e-03 -3.043863e-03  1.477910e-04  5.309158e-03 -2.284709e-02
##  [4,] -3.549595e-03 -4.562212e-03 -3.638964e-05 -2.664336e-04 -1.033467e-02
##  [5,] -4.904397e-03  1.123887e-04 -1.299437e-03  3.222237e-01  4.260356e-02
##  [6,]  8.121917e-04 -1.580815e-03  4.430682e-04  4.342993e-01  6.475172e-02
##  [7,] -2.215982e-03 -8.331929e-04  1.633600e-03  2.281973e-01  2.564996e-02
##  [8,]  3.560643e-05  8.921600e-05  5.898275e-03  1.075645e-01  4.821292e-03
##  [9,] -5.736124e-04 -5.423584e-03  2.496149e-03  4.161040e-02 -1.708765e-04

## [11,] -3.241384e-03 -1.775920e-03 -1.117891e-03  6.183705e-03 -1.296303e-02
## [12,] -4.108631e-03  3.542084e-04  1.975361e-03  3.930077e-03 -1.640076e-03
## [13,]  3.990742e-04 -1.341341e-05 -1.149817e-04 -3.264844e-05  8.420805e-05
## [14,]  1.750761e-02  4.278619e-03 -4.683494e-03 -1.345988e-03  2.795512e-03

## [16,]  9.095902e-02  2.179218e-02 -1.045092e-02 -1.301762e-03  6.186913e-03
## [17,]  1.136901e-01  1.537511e-02 -2.048034e-02 -5.072416e-04  8.879143e-03
## [18,]  1.605718e-01  3.208097e-02 -1.719832e-02  6.961187e-04  9.883292e-03
## [19,]  1.679849e-01  7.934056e-03 -2.642022e-02 -3.180460e-03  9.534979e-03
##               [,6]          [,7]          [,8]          [,9]         [,10]
##  [1,] -0.166310429 -1.070397e-01 -3.951624e-01 -1.133984e-02 -1.250006e-01
##  [2,]  0.000344255 -4.640634e-02 -1.213737e-01 -4.914758e-03 -1.388417e-01
##  [3,] -0.000310927 -1.988322e-03 -5.214693e-03 -1.355966e-03 -3.890870e-02
##  [4,]  0.000000000 -2.105357e-03 -1.733495e-03 -9.379771e-04 -1.998163e-02
##  [5,]  0.000000000 -3.412145e-03  4.302977e-03 -4.859875e-03 -2.527745e-02
##  [6,]  0.000000000  2.007568e-03  1.003319e-03  6.914941e-04 -2.283676e-02
##  [7,]  0.000000000  4.442060e-03 -6.403657e-03 -2.379512e-03 -2.221838e-02
##  [8,]  0.000000000  5.588477e-03  5.662638e-04  1.703093e-03 -9.136475e-03
##  [9,]  0.000000000  6.701052e-03  2.149837e-03 -4.235227e-03 -9.453558e-03
## [10,]  0.000000000  2.364608e-03  1.398058e-03 -2.197012e-03 -7.589776e-03
## [11,]  0.000000000  1.950614e-03 -7.080209e-03 -1.372766e-03  6.448593e-03
## [12,]  0.000000000  4.252656e-04  3.105674e-04 -1.123800e-04 -1.977149e-03
## [13,]  0.000000000  4.408772e-06  2.303441e-05 -9.379166e-06  9.471989e-05
## [14,]  0.000000000  2.245458e-04  3.494706e-03  1.496622e-03  3.301554e-03
## [15,]  0.000000000  7.077362e-03  8.633612e-03  5.111443e-03  1.118271e-02
## [16,]  0.000000000  6.172316e-03  1.396928e-02  5.946498e-03  2.469919e-02
## [17,]  0.000000000  1.656542e-02  2.115373e-02  9.507916e-03  2.726955e-02
## [18,]  0.000000000  2.787245e-02  3.517065e-02  1.916912e-02  5.008624e-02
## [19,]  0.000000000  5.884369e-03  3.425985e-02  1.108882e-02  7.128642e-02
#
stepwise.results.sd <- 
  stepwise_replacement(
    sdfrommxc, 
    pars1 = c(COD1), 
    pars2 = c(COD2), 
    direction = dir.pick)
dim(stepwise.results.sd) <- dim(COD1)
stepwise.results.sd
##                [,1]          [,2]          [,3]          [,4]          [,5]

## [18,]  0.0000000000  2.772052e-02  3.498382e-02  1.914552e-02  5.017712e-02

Sanity check with the total difference

original.sd <- sdfrommxc(COD2) - sdfrommxc(COD1)

cat('\nThe difference between the sum of the decomposed contributions and the original difference is:', sum(horiuchi.results.sd) - original.sd, ' with the N used in the decomposition is', N.input)
## 
## The difference between the sum of the decomposed contributions and the original difference is: -1.249747e-08  with the N used in the decomposition is 1000
cat('\nThe difference between the sum of the stepwise replacement decomposed contributions and the original difference is:', sum(stepwise.results.sd) - original.sd, ' with the direction used in the decomposition is', dir.pick)
## 
## The difference between the sum of the stepwise replacement decomposed contributions and the original difference is: 0  with the direction used in the decomposition is both

Do some data handling to have a nice dataset

horiuchi.results.sd            <- data.frame(horiuchi.results.sd)
colnames(horiuchi.results.sd)  <- cause_names
horiuchi.results.sd$Age        <- age_names
horiuchi.results.sd            <- gather(data = horiuchi.results.sd,
                                         key = Cause,
                                         value = Contribution,-Age) 

stepwise.results.sd            <- data.frame(stepwise.results.sd)
colnames(stepwise.results.sd)  <- cause_names
stepwise.results.sd$Age        <- age_names
stepwise.results.sd            <- gather(data = stepwise.results.sd,
                                         key = Cause,
                                         value = Contribution,
                                         -Age)

Visualisation

p.horiuchi.sd <- 
  ggplot(data = horiuchi.results.sd, 
         aes(x = factor(Age, 
                        levels = unique(horiuchi.results.sd$Age)), 
             y = Contribution,
             fill = factor(Cause,
                           levels = unique(horiuchi.results.sd$Cause)))) +
  ggtitle(bquote(~'Change in '~ sd[0] ~'1996-2013, Horiuchi replacement method' ))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  labs(x = "Age group", y = "Contribution", fill = "Cause") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(order = 1, ncol = 3)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust=1),
    axis.line        = element_blank(),
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 8),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )



p.stepwise.sd <- 
  ggplot(data = stepwise.results.sd, 
         aes(x = factor(Age, 
                        levels = unique(stepwise.results.sd$Age)), 
             y = Contribution,
             fill = factor(Cause, 
                           levels = unique(stepwise.results.sd$Cause)))) +
  ggtitle(bquote(~'Change in '~ sd[0] ~'1996-2013, Stepwise replacement method' ))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  labs(x = "Age group", y = "Contribution", fill = "Cause") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(order = 1, ncol = 3)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust=1),
    axis.line        = element_blank(),
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 8),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )

Comparison of the two methods

p.both.sd <-  ggpubr::ggarrange(p.horiuchi.sd,
                             p.stepwise.sd,
                             ncol = 1, 
                             labels = c('a', 'b'),
                             common.legend = TRUE,
                             legend = 'bottom',
                             align = 'hv',
                             widths = c(1,1),
                             heights = c(1,1))
p.both.sd
